data.title: OpenAI Faces Scrutiny Over Handling of ChatGPT Account Linked to Mass Shooting
data.description: Sam Altman has issued an apology following OpenAI's failure to notify law enforcement about a ChatGPT account associated with the suspect in the February Tumbler Ridge mass shooting, which resulted in eight fatalities. According to NS3.AI, OpenAI stated that the account was banned in June 2025 due to activities related to promoting violence. However, the company determined that the situation did not meet its criteria for a credible or imminent threat of serious physical harm.

data.title: Ripple Expands Offerings with RLUSD and Custody Services
data.description: Ripple has broadened its range of services beyond its well-known cryptocurrency, XRP. According to NS3.AI, the company now includes the XRP Ledger, RippleNet, the RLUSD stablecoin, and custody services for financial institutions in its portfolio. The RLUSD stablecoin was introduced in December 2024, and DBS has been identified as a client for Ripple's custody services.

data.title: Benchmark Sets $3 Price Target for DDC Enterprise
data.description: Benchmark has assigned a Buy rating to DDC Enterprise, setting a price target of $3. According to NS3.AI, DDC Enterprise reported holding 2,383 BTC as of April 21 and has set a goal to increase its holdings to 5,000 BTC by the end of this year.

data.title: Israeli Defense Minister Rules Out Ceasefire in Lebanon Amid Ongoing Attacks
data.description: Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant stated on the 27th that a ceasefire in Lebanon is not feasible while Israeli forces continue to face attacks. According to Odaily, Gallant made these remarks during a meeting with Joanna Wronecka, the United Nations Special Coordinator for Lebanon. He also issued a warning to Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem, accusing him of 'playing with fire.' Gallant emphasized that the Lebanese government must ensure Hezbollah is disarmed first in the region south of the Litani River and subsequently throughout Lebanon.

data.title: Israeli Defense Minister: Israeli forces are still under attack, a ceasefire in Lebanon is impossible.
data.description: On the 27th local time, Israeli Defense Minister Katz, during a meeting with UN Special Coordinator for Lebanon Prashte, stated that a ceasefire in Lebanon was impossible while Israeli forces were still under attack. He also warned Hezbollah leader Qassem Soleimani that he was "playing with fire." He demanded that the Lebanese government ensure Hezbollah first disarmed south of the Litani River, and then disarmed throughout Lebanon. (CCTV) (Jinshi Data APP)

data.title: Avalanche Foundation: Supporting DeFi United to restore rsETH collateral support
data.description: The Avalanche Foundation stated on the X platform that it supports DeFi United, a coordinated effort to restore rsETH staking support following the April 18th incident. DeFi is undergoing public stress testing, with transparent ledgers and genuine accountability, whereas traditional financial systems typically require months or years to complete such repairs. The Avalanche Foundation expressed its support for Aave and the broader DeFi ecosystem.

data.title: Block Unveils New Bitcoin Initiatives Across Platforms
data.description:
Block has introduced a series of new bitcoin products across its Bitkey, Cash App, and Square platforms. According to NS3.AI, one of the key features is a 5% Bitcoin Back program available on eligible purchases made with Square merchants. Additionally, Cash App has increased its bitcoin withdrawal limits to $10,000 per day and $25,000 per week.

Block has also implemented a Proof of Reserves system, allowing the public to verify bitcoin balances for its corporate treasury and customer holdings across Cash App and Square. Looking ahead, the company plans to showcase NFC tap-to-pay bitcoin transactions on Square at the Bitcoin Las Vegas 2026 event, with zero processing fees through 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs have seen net inflows for nine consecutive days, with a cumulative inflow of approximately $2.1 billion.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data monitoring, Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded net inflows for the ninth consecutive trading day on April 24, with a single-day inflow of $14.45 million. This round of continuous inflows has accumulated to approximately $2.1 billion, setting a record for the longest net inflow since September 2025. Last week, ETFs saw a cumulative inflow of $823.7 million, with BlackRock's IBIT seeing a weekly inflow of $983 million, a new high in nearly six months. Ki Young Ju, founder of CryptoQuant, stated that the Bitcoin market is currently driven by futures, with open interest continuing to rise, but apart from ETF inflows and MicroStrategy purchases, on-chain apparent demand remains negative. The chief analyst of CEX.IO pointed out that the recent rise is clearly driven by short covering, with the total amount of short liquidation since April 13 reaching approximately $2.8 billion, far exceeding the $1.8 billion of long liquidation. Some ETF demand may stem from term arbitrage trading, which involves buying IBIT and shorting CME futures to profit from the price difference. This strategy is market-neutral and not purely bullish. Currently, the options market's 25-delta skew is in negative territory, indicating that investors are paying a premium for downside protection.

data.title: Stablecoin B2B Payments Projected to Hit $5 Trillion by 2035
data.description: Cross-border B2B stablecoin payments are expected to reach $5 trillion by 2035, according to Juniper Research. The report highlights that B2B transactions will constitute 85% of the total stablecoin transaction value by that year.

data.title: Futures Liquidations Surge to $449 Million in 24 Hours
data.description: Futures liquidations have seen a significant increase, with $153 million liquidated in the past hour alone. According to NS3.AI, the total futures liquidations have reached $449 million over the past 24 hours. This surge in liquidations reflects heightened market volatility and investor caution.

data.title: Iran Criticizes U.S. Diplomacy in Talks with Russia
data.description: Iran's Foreign Minister has conveyed to Russian President Vladimir Putin that the United States' 'destructive habits,' 'unreasonable demands,' and frequent changes in stance are hindering diplomatic progress. According to Odaily, these remarks highlight ongoing tensions in international negotiations.

data.title: Fidelity Digital Assets Report Highlights Stabilizing Trends in Crypto Market
data.description:
Fidelity Digital Assets has released its Q2 2026 Signal Report, indicating signs of stabilization in the crypto market despite a consolidating phase at the beginning of the quarter. According to ChainCatcher, the report highlights Bitcoin's increasing dominance and a concentration of capital in the most liquid assets. These trends, along with unrealized profit levels and momentum indicators, align with characteristics of a correction period, potentially laying the groundwork for a more stable market structure.

The report also notes a divergence between network usage and price trends for Ethereum and Solana, suggesting robust demand at the protocol level. Additionally, the persistent negative funding rates in Bitcoin futures are interpreted by research firm 10x as indicative of institutional hedging strategies rather than a bearish market outlook.

data.title: Iran's Foreign Ministry: The United States' "destructive habits," "unreasonable demands," and frequent changes in position are slowing down diplomatic progress.
data.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry: The Iranian Foreign Minister told Russian President Putin that the United States' "destructive habits," "unreasonable demands," and frequent changes in position are slowing down diplomatic progress. (Jinshi)

data.title: China Orders Meta to Reverse $2 Billion Acquisition of AI Startup Manus
data.description: China's National Development and Reform Commission has directed Meta to reverse its acquisition of AI startup Manus, valued at approximately $2 billion. According to NS3.AI, the decision follows an investigation initiated by China's commerce ministry in January 2026. During the review process, Manus co-founders were prohibited from leaving China.

data.title: Net institutional demand for Bitcoin is growing at its fastest pace since the end of 2025.
data.description: According to Omar Faridi's monitoring, net demand from institutional investors for Bitcoin has regained strong momentum, reaching its highest growth rate since the end of 2025. Data from Bloomberg, Glassnode, and Bitwise Europe shows that buying pressure from professional institutions such as funds and asset management companies significantly outweighs selling pressure. Industry analysts point out that institutional funds are typically characterized by large-scale transactions and long-term holdings, and this renewed acceleration in net demand indicates increased confidence among large investors in Bitcoin as a store of value. The current increase in institutional participation is driving improved market liquidity and is seen as a signal of further consolidation of Bitcoin's position in the global financial system.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 77,000 USDT with a 1.48% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 27, 2026, 15:19 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 77,000 USDT and is now trading at 76,969.132813 USDT, with a narrowed 1.48% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 620 USDT with a 2.14% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 27, 2026, 15:23 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 620 USDT and is now trading at 618.799988 USDT, with a narrowed 2.14% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Solana Foundation Advances Quantum-Resistant Measures
data.description:
Solana Foundation has detailed its progress in preparing for quantum computing threats. According to Foresight News, the foundation's two main validator client development teams, Anza and Firedancer, have independently concluded that the post-quantum digital signature scheme Falcon is the preferred migration path. Both teams have completed initial implementations, with the code publicly available on their respective GitHub repositories. Additionally, Blueshift's Solana Winternitz Vault has been deployed for over two years and is one of the few quantum-resistant primitives in mainstream blockchain use, having been cited in Google's quantum AI white paper earlier this year.

The Solana Foundation stated that while quantum computing poses a substantial threat, it will take several years to materialize. There is no immediate need for migration, but research, infrastructure, and ecosystem coordination are ready to enable a swift transition when necessary. The foundation anticipates that this will not significantly impact network performance.

data.title: Oil Prices Surge with Notable Daily Gains
data.description: Oil prices experienced significant increases, with both Brent and WTI crude oil seeing their daily gains expand to 2%. According to Odaily, the latest prices were reported at $98.92 per barrel for WTI and $102 per barrel for Brent.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Basic Metals Face High-Level Reassessment Amid Supply and Demand Shift
data.description: The basic metals sector is undergoing a significant reassessment as expectations have overheated and supply and demand dynamics shift. According to Jin10, the recent geopolitical advantages enjoyed by the sector may be coming to an end. Aluminum companies are ramping up production, visible copper inventories are nearing one million tons, and zinc mines are accelerating output. These developments are contributing to a challenging environment for the basic metals market.
